title: PM Process (Basic)
summary: Basic PM process: identify stakeholders, goals, objectives, deliverables, scope statement and risk management plan.
content_html:
PM Process (Basic)

  1. Choose PM method (Agile/Waterfall)
  2. Identify Stakeholders
  3. Determine Project Goal
  4. Identify Project Objectives (SMART)
  5. Identify Deliverables and Success Criteria
  6. Prepare Project Scope Statement
  7. Create a Risk Management Plan
  8. Use Milestones

Stakeholders

  • Customer: the person who has the problem to solve, sponsors the project, approves deliverables
  • Project Sponsor: support the project, prioritize objectives
  • Functional Managers: manage departments and team members
  • Team Members: carry out project work

SMART Objectives

Objectives must: Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Realistic, Time-related.

Risk Management

Risk Score = Probability (1-5) × Impact (1-5). Plan for risks has score ≥ 9 (medium or higher).

Response actions: Accept (low risk), Avoid (change scope), Mitigate (reduce impact), Transfer (insurance).

Milestones

Milestone marks important progress of the project. All tasks that depend on a milestone will automatically be rescheduled when the milestone changes.
